name: apoc.coll.indexOf
text: Returns the index for the first occurrence of the specified value in the `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.coll.insert
text: Inserts a value into the specified index in the `LIST<ANY>`.
signature: apoc.coll.insert(coll :: LIST<ANY>, index :: INTEGER, value :: ANY) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.insertAll
text: Inserts all of the values into the `LIST<ANY>`, starting at the specified index.
signature: apoc.coll.insertAll(coll :: LIST<ANY>, index :: INTEGER, values :: LIST<ANY>) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.intersection
text: Returns the distinct intersection of two `LIST<ANY>` values.

name: apoc.coll.isEqualCollection
text: Returns true if the two collections contain the same elements with the same cardinality in any order.

name: apoc.coll.max
text: Returns the maximum of all values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.coll.min
text: Returns the minimum of all values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.coll.occurrences
text: Returns the count of the given item in the collection.

name: apoc.coll.pairs
text: Returns a `LIST<ANY>` of adjacent elements in the `LIST<ANY>` ([1,2],[2,3],[3,null]).

name: apoc.coll.pairsMin
text: Returns `LIST<ANY>` values of adjacent elements in the `LIST<ANY>` ([1,2],[2,3]), skipping the final element.

name: apoc.coll.partition
text:
Partitions the original `LIST<ANY>` into a new `LIST<ANY>` of the given batch size.
The final `LIST<ANY>` may be smaller than the given batch size.

name: apoc.coll.randomItem
text: Returns a random item from the `LIST<ANY>`, or null on `LIST<NOTHING>` or `LIST<NULL>`.

name: apoc.coll.randomItems
text: Returns a `LIST<ANY>` of `itemCount` random items from the original `LIST<ANY>` (optionally allowing elements in the original `LIST<ANY>` to be selected more than once).
signature: apoc.coll.randomItems(coll :: LIST<ANY>, itemCount :: INTEGER, allowRepick = false :: BOOLEAN) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.remove
text: Removes a range of values from the `LIST<ANY>`, beginning at position index for the given length of values.
signature: apoc.coll.remove(coll :: LIST<ANY>, index :: INTEGER, length = 1 :: INTEGER) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.removeAll
text: Returns the first `LIST<ANY>` with all elements also present in the second `LIST<ANY>` removed.

name: apoc.coll.runningTotal
text: Returns an accumulative `LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>`.

name: apoc.coll.set
text: Sets the element at the given index to the new value.
signature: apoc.coll.set(coll :: LIST<ANY>, index :: INTEGER, value :: ANY) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.shuffle
text: Returns the `LIST<ANY>` shuffled.

name: apoc.coll.sort
text: Sorts the given `LIST<ANY>` into ascending order.

name: apoc.coll.sortMaps
text: Sorts the given `LIST<MAP<STRING, ANY>>` into descending order, based on the `MAP` property indicated by `prop`.

name: apoc.coll.sortMulti
text:
Sorts the given `LIST<MAP<STRING, ANY>>` by the given fields.
To indicate that a field should be sorted according to ascending values, prefix it with a caret (^).
It is also possible to add limits to the `LIST<MAP<STRING, ANY>>` and to skip values.
signature: apoc.coll.sortMulti(coll :: LIST<MAP>, orderFields = [] :: LIST<STRING>, limit = -1 :: INTEGER, skip = 0 :: INTEGER) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.coll.sortNodes
text: Sorts the given `LIST<NODE>` by the property of the nodes into descending order.

name: apoc.coll.sortText
text: Sorts the given `LIST<STRING>` into ascending order.

name: apoc.coll.subtract
text: Returns the first `LIST<ANY>` as a set with all the elements of the second `LIST<ANY>` removed.

name: apoc.coll.sum
text: Returns the sum of all the `INTEGER | FLOAT` in the `LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>`.

name: apoc.coll.sumLongs
text: Returns the sum of all the `INTEGER | FLOAT` in the `LIST<INTEGER | FLOAT>`.

name: apoc.coll.toSet
text: Returns a unique `LIST<ANY>` from the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.coll.union
text: Returns the distinct union of the two given `LIST<ANY>` values.

name: apoc.coll.unionAll
text: Returns the full union of the two given `LIST<ANY>` values (duplicates included).

name: apoc.coll.zip
text: Returns the two given `LIST<ANY>` values zipped together as a `LIST<LIST<ANY>>`.

name: apoc.convert.fromJsonList
text: Converts the given JSON list into a Cypher `LIST<STRING>`.
signature: apoc.convert.fromJsonList(list :: STRING, path =  :: STRING, pathOptions = null :: LIST<STRING>) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.convert.fromJsonMap
text: Converts the given JSON map into a Cypher `MAP`.
signature: apoc.convert.fromJsonMap(map :: STRING, path =  :: STRING, pathOptions = null :: LIST<STRING>) :: MAP

name: apoc.convert.getJsonProperty
text: Converts a serialized JSON object from the property of the given `NODE` into the equivalent Cypher structure (e.g. `MAP`, `LIST<ANY>`).
signature: apoc.convert.getJsonProperty(node :: NODE, key :: STRING, path =  :: STRING, pathOptions = null :: LIST<STRING>) :: ANY

name: apoc.convert.getJsonPropertyMap
text: Converts a serialized JSON object from the property of the given `NODE` into a Cypher `MAP`.
signature: apoc.convert.getJsonPropertyMap(node :: NODE, key :: STRING, path =  :: STRING, pathOptions = null :: LIST<STRING>) :: MAP

name: apoc.convert.toJson
text: Serializes the given JSON value.

name: apoc.convert.toList
text: Converts the given value into a `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.convert.toMap
text: Converts the given value into a `MAP`.

name: apoc.convert.toNode
text: Converts the given value into a `NODE`.

name: apoc.convert.toNodeList
text: Converts the given value into a `LIST<NODE>`.

name: apoc.convert.toRelationship
text: Converts the given value into a `RELATIONSHIP`.

name: apoc.convert.toRelationshipList
text: Converts the given value into a `LIST<RELATIONSHIP>`.

name: apoc.convert.toSet
text: Converts the given value into a set represented in Cypher as a `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.convert.toSortedJsonMap
text: Converts a serialized JSON object from the property of a given `NODE` into a Cypher `MAP`.
signature: apoc.convert.toSortedJsonMap(value :: ANY, ignoreCase = true :: BOOLEAN) :: STRING

name: apoc.create.uuid
text: Returns a UUID.

name: apoc.create.vNode
text: Returns a virtual `NODE`.

name: apoc.create.vRelationship
text: Returns a virtual `RELATIONSHIP`.
signature: apoc.create.vRelationship(from :: NODE, relType :: STRING, props :: MAP, to :: NODE) :: RELATIONSHIP

name: apoc.create.virtual.fromNode
text: Returns a virtual `NODE` from the given existing `NODE`. The virtual `NODE` only contains the requested properties.
signature: apoc.create.virtual.fromNode(node :: NODE, propertyNames :: LIST<STRING>, config = {} :: MAP) :: NODE

name: apoc.cypher.runFirstColumnMany
text: Runs the given statement with the given parameters and returns the first column collected into a `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.cypher.runFirstColumnSingle
text: Runs the given statement with the given parameters and returns the first element of the first column.

name: apoc.data.url
text: Turns a URL into a `MAP`.

name: apoc.date.add
text: Adds a unit of specified time to the given timestamp.
signature: apoc.date.add(time :: INTEGER, unit :: STRING, addValue :: INTEGER, addUnit :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.date.convert
text: Converts the given timestamp from one time unit into a timestamp of a different time unit.
signature: apoc.date.convert(time :: INTEGER, unit :: STRING, toUnit :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.date.convertFormat
text: Converts a `STRING` of one type of date format into a `STRING` of another type of date format.
signature: apoc.date.convertFormat(temporal :: STRING, currentFormat :: STRING, convertTo = yyyy-MM-dd :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.date.currentTimestamp
text: Returns the current Unix epoch timestamp in milliseconds.

name: apoc.date.field
text: Returns the value of one field from the given date time.
signature: apoc.date.field(time :: INTEGER, unit = d :: STRING, timezone = UTC :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.date.fields
text: Splits the given date into fields returning a `MAP` containing the values of each field.
signature: apoc.date.fields(date :: STRING, pattern = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss :: STRING) :: MAP

name: apoc.date.format
text:
Returns a `STRING` representation of the time value.
The time unit (default: ms), date format (default: ISO), and time zone (default: current time zone) can all be changed.
signature: apoc.date.format(time :: INTEGER, unit = ms :: STRING, format = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss :: STRING, timezone =  :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.date.fromISO8601
text: Converts the given date `STRING` (ISO8601) to an `INTEGER` representing the time value in milliseconds.

name: apoc.date.parse
text: Parses the given date `STRING` from a specified format into the specified time unit.
signature: apoc.date.parse(time :: STRING, unit = ms :: STRING, format = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss :: STRING, timezone =  :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.date.systemTimezone
text: Returns the display name of the system time zone (e.g. Europe/London).

name: apoc.date.toISO8601
text: Returns a `STRING` representation of a specified time value in the ISO8601 format.
signature: apoc.date.toISO8601(time :: INTEGER, unit = ms :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.date.toYears
text: Converts the given timestamp or the given date into a `FLOAT` representing years.
signature: apoc.date.toYears(value :: ANY, format = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss :: STRING) :: FLOAT

name: apoc.diff.nodes
text: Returns a `MAP` detailing the differences between the two given `NODE` values.

name: apoc.hashing.fingerprint
text:
Calculates a MD5 checksum over a `NODE` or `RELATIONSHIP` (identical entities share the same checksum).
Unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.

name: apoc.hashing.fingerprintGraph
text:
Calculates a MD5 checksum over the full graph.
This function uses in-memory data structures.
Unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.

name: apoc.hashing.fingerprinting
text:
Calculates a MD5 checksum over a `NODE` or `RELATIONSHIP` (identical entities share the same checksum).
Unlike `apoc.hashing.fingerprint()`, this function supports a number of config parameters.
Unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.

name: apoc.json.path
text: Returns the given JSON path.
signature: apoc.json.path(json :: STRING, path = $ :: STRING, pathOptions = null :: LIST<STRING>) :: ANY

name: apoc.label.exists
text: Returns true or false depending on whether or not the given label exists.

name: apoc.map.clean
text: Filters the keys and values contained in the given `LIST<ANY>` values.
signature: apoc.map.clean(map :: MAP, keys :: LIST<STRING>, values :: LIST<ANY>) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.flatten
text:
Flattens nested items in the given `MAP`.
This function is the reverse of the `apoc.map.unflatten` function.

name: apoc.map.fromLists
text: Creates a `MAP` from the keys and values in the given `LIST<ANY>` values.

name: apoc.map.fromNodes
text: Returns a `MAP` of the given prop to the node of the given label.

name: apoc.map.fromPairs
text: Creates a `MAP` from the given `LIST<LIST<ANY>>` of key-value pairs.

name: apoc.map.fromValues
text: Creates a `MAP` from the alternating keys and values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.map.get
text:
Returns a value for the given key.
If the given key does not exist, or lacks a default value, this function will throw an exception.
signature: apoc.map.get(map :: MAP, key :: STRING, value = null :: ANY, fail = true :: BOOLEAN) :: ANY

name: apoc.map.groupBy
text: Creates a `MAP` of the `LIST<ANY>` keyed by the given property, with single values.

name: apoc.map.groupByMulti
text: Creates a `MAP` of the `LIST<ANY>` values keyed by the given property, with the `LIST<ANY>` values.

name: apoc.map.merge
text: Merges the two given `MAP` values into one `MAP`.

name: apoc.map.mergeList
text: Merges all `MAP` values in the given `LIST<MAP<STRING, ANY>>` into one `MAP`.

name: apoc.map.mget
text:
Returns a `LIST<ANY>` for the given keys.
If one of the keys does not exist, or lacks a default value, this function will throw an exception.
signature: apoc.map.mget(map :: MAP, keys :: LIST<STRING>, values = [] :: LIST<ANY>, fail = true :: BOOLEAN) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.map.removeKey
text: Removes the given key from the `MAP` (recursively if recursive is true).
signature: apoc.map.removeKey(map :: MAP, key :: STRING, config = {} :: MAP) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.removeKeys
text: Removes the given keys from the `MAP` (recursively if recursive is true).
signature: apoc.map.removeKeys(map :: MAP, keys :: LIST<STRING>, config = {} :: MAP) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.setEntry
text: Adds or updates the given entry in the `MAP`.
signature: apoc.map.setEntry(map :: MAP, key :: STRING, value :: ANY) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.setKey
text: Adds or updates the given entry in the `MAP`.
signature: apoc.map.setKey(map :: MAP, key :: STRING, value :: ANY) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.setLists
text: Adds or updates the given keys/value pairs provided in `LIST<ANY>` format (e.g. [key1, key2],[value1, value2]) in a `MAP`.
signature: apoc.map.setLists(map :: MAP, keys :: LIST<STRING>, values :: LIST<ANY>) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.setPairs
text: Adds or updates the given key/value pairs (e.g. [key1,value1],[key2,value2]) in a `MAP`.

name: apoc.map.setValues
text: Adds or updates the alternating key/value pairs (e.g. [key1,value1,key2,value2]) in a `MAP`.

name: apoc.map.sortedProperties
text:
Returns a `LIST<ANY>` of key/value pairs.
The pairs are sorted by alphabetically by key, with optional case sensitivity.
signature: apoc.map.sortedProperties(map :: MAP, ignoreCase = true :: BOOLEAN) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.map.submap
text:
Returns a sub-map for the given keys.
If one of the keys does not exist, or lacks a default value, this function will throw an exception.
signature: apoc.map.submap(map :: MAP, keys :: LIST<STRING>, values = [] :: LIST<ANY>, fail = true :: BOOLEAN) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.unflatten
text:
Unflattens items in the given `MAP` to nested items.
This function is the reverse of the `apoc.map.flatten` function.

name: apoc.map.updateTree
text: Adds the data `MAP` on each level of the nested tree, where the key-value pairs match.
signature: apoc.map.updateTree(tree :: MAP, key :: STRING, data :: LIST<LIST<ANY>>) :: MAP

name: apoc.map.values
text: Returns a `LIST<ANY>` indicated by the given keys (returns a null value if a given key is missing).
signature: apoc.map.values(map :: MAP, keys = [] :: LIST<STRING>, addNullsForMissing = false :: BOOLEAN) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.math.maxByte
text: Returns the maximum value of a byte.

name: apoc.math.maxDouble
text: Returns the largest positive finite value of type double.

name: apoc.math.maxInt
text: Returns the maximum value of an integer.

name: apoc.math.maxLong
text: Returns the maximum value of a long.

name: apoc.math.minByte
text: Returns the minimum value of a byte.

name: apoc.math.minDouble
text: Returns the smallest positive non-zero value of type double.

name: apoc.math.minInt
text: Returns the minimum value of an integer.

name: apoc.math.minLong
text: Returns the minimum value of a long.

name: apoc.math.sigmoid
text: Returns the sigmoid of the given value.

name: apoc.math.sigmoidPrime
text: Returns the sigmoid prime [ sigmoid(val) * (1 - sigmoid(val)) ] of the given value.

name: apoc.meta.cypher.isType
text: Returns true if the given value matches the given type.

name: apoc.meta.cypher.type
text: Returns the type name of the given value.

name: apoc.meta.cypher.types
text: Returns a `MAP` containing the type names of the given values.

name: apoc.meta.nodes.count
text: Returns the sum of the `NODE` values with the given labels in the `LIST<STRING>`.

name: apoc.node.degree
text: Returns the total degrees of the given `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.degree.in
text: Returns the total number of incoming `RELATIONSHIP` values connected to the given `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.degree.out
text: Returns the total number of outgoing `RELATIONSHIP` values from the given `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.id
text: Returns the id for the given virtual `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.labels
text: Returns the labels for the given virtual `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.relationship.exists
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` based on whether the given `NODE` has a connecting `RELATIONSHIP` (or whether the given `NODE` has a connecting `RELATIONSHIP` of the given type and direction).

name: apoc.node.relationship.types
text: Returns a `LIST<STRING>` of distinct `RELATIONSHIP` types for the given `NODE`.

name: apoc.node.relationships.exist
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` based on whether the given `NODE` has connecting `RELATIONSHIP` values (or whether the given `NODE` has connecting `RELATIONSHIP` values of the given type and direction).

name: apoc.nodes.connected
text:
Returns true when a given `NODE` is directly connected to another given `NODE`.
This function is optimized for dense nodes.
signature: apoc.nodes.connected(startNode :: NODE, endNode :: NODE, types =  :: STRING) :: BOOLEAN

name: apoc.nodes.isDense
text: Returns true if the given `NODE` is a dense node.

name: apoc.nodes.relationship.types
text: Returns a `LIST<STRING>` of distinct `RELATIONSHIP` types from the given `LIST<NODE>` values.

name: apoc.nodes.relationships.exist
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` based on whether or not the given `NODE` values have the given `RELATIONSHIP` values.

name: apoc.number.arabicToRoman
text: Converts the given Arabic numbers to Roman numbers.

name: apoc.number.exact.add
text: Returns the result of adding the two given large numbers (using Java BigDecimal).

name: apoc.number.exact.div
text: Returns the result of dividing a given large number with another given large number (using Java BigDecimal).
signature: apoc.number.exact.div(stringA :: STRING, stringB :: STRING, precision = 0 :: INTEGER, roundingMode = HALF_UP :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.number.exact.mul
text: Returns the result of multiplying two given large numbers (using Java BigDecimal).
signature: apoc.number.exact.mul(stringA :: STRING, stringB :: STRING, precision = 0 :: INTEGER, roundingMode = HALF_UP :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.number.exact.sub
text: Returns the result of subtracting a given large number from another given large number (using Java BigDecimal).

name: apoc.number.exact.toExact
text: Returns the exact value of the given number (using Java BigDecimal).

name: apoc.number.exact.toFloat
text: Returns the `FLOAT` of the given large number (using Java BigDecimal).
signature: apoc.number.exact.toFloat(string :: STRING, precision = 0 :: INTEGER, roundingMode = HALF_UP :: STRING) :: FLOAT

name: apoc.number.exact.toInteger
text: Returns the `INTEGER` of the given large number (using Java BigDecimal).
signature: apoc.number.exact.toInteger(string :: STRING, precision = 0 :: INTEGER, roundingMode = HALF_UP :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.number.format
text: Formats the given `INTEGER` or `FLOAT` using the given pattern and language to produce a `STRING`.
signature: apoc.number.format(number :: ANY, pattern =  :: STRING, language =  :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.number.parseFloat
text: Parses the given `STRING` using the given pattern and language to produce a `FLOAT`.
signature: apoc.number.parseFloat(text :: STRING, pattern =  :: STRING, language =  :: STRING) :: FLOAT

name: apoc.number.parseInt
text: Parses the given `STRING` using the given pattern and language to produce a `INTEGER`.
signature: apoc.number.parseInt(text :: STRING, pattern =  :: STRING, language =  :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.number.romanToArabic
text: Converts the given Roman numbers to Arabic numbers.

name: apoc.path.combine
text: Combines the two given `PATH` values into one `PATH`.

name: apoc.path.create
text: Returns a `PATH` from the given start `NODE` and `LIST<RELATIONSHIP>`.

name: apoc.path.elements
text: Converts the given `PATH` into a `LIST<NODE | RELATIONSHIP>`.

name: apoc.path.slice
text: Returns a new `PATH` of the given length, taken from the given `PATH` at the given offset.
signature: apoc.path.slice(path :: PATH, offset = 0 :: INTEGER, length = -1 :: INTEGER) :: PATH

name: apoc.rel.endNode
text: Returns the end `NODE` for the given virtual `RELATIONSHIP`.

name: apoc.rel.id
text: Returns the id for the given virtual `RELATIONSHIP`.

name: apoc.rel.startNode
text: Returns the start `NODE` for the given virtual `RELATIONSHIP`.

name: apoc.rel.type
text: Returns the type for the given virtual `RELATIONSHIP`.

name: apoc.schema.node.constraintExists
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` depending on whether or not a constraint exists for the given `NODE` label with the given property names.

name: apoc.schema.node.indexExists
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` depending on whether or not an index exists for the given `NODE` label with the given property names.

name: apoc.schema.relationship.constraintExists
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` depending on whether or not a constraint exists for the given `RELATIONSHIP` type with the given property names.

name: apoc.schema.relationship.indexExists
text: Returns a `BOOLEAN` depending on whether or not an index exists for the given `RELATIONSHIP` type with the given property names.

name: apoc.scoring.existence
text: Returns the given score if true, 0 if false.

name: apoc.scoring.pareto
text: Applies a Pareto scoring function over the given `INTEGER` values.
signature: apoc.scoring.pareto(minimumThreshold :: INTEGER, eightyPercentValue :: INTEGER, maximumValue :: INTEGER, score :: INTEGER) :: FLOAT

name: apoc.temporal.format
text: Formats the given temporal value into the given time format.
signature: apoc.temporal.format(temporal :: ANY, format = yyyy-MM-dd :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.temporal.formatDuration
text: Formats the given duration into the given time format.

name: apoc.temporal.toZonedTemporal
text: Parses the given date `STRING` using the specified format into the given time zone.
signature: apoc.temporal.toZonedTemporal(time :: STRING, format = yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss :: STRING, timezone = UTC :: STRING) :: ZONED DATETIME

name: apoc.text.base64Decode
text: Decodes the given Base64 encoded `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.base64Encode
text: Encodes the given `STRING` with Base64.

name: apoc.text.base64UrlDecode
text: Decodes the given Base64 encoded URL.

name: apoc.text.base64UrlEncode
text: Encodes the given URL with Base64.

name: apoc.text.byteCount
text: Returns the size of the given `STRING` in bytes.
signature: apoc.text.byteCount(text :: STRING, charset = UTF-8 :: STRING) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.text.bytes
text: Returns the given `STRING` as bytes.
signature: apoc.text.bytes(text :: STRING, charset = UTF-8 :: STRING) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.text.camelCase
text: Converts the given `STRING` to camel case.

name: apoc.text.capitalize
text: Capitalizes the first letter of the given `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.capitalizeAll
text: Capitalizes the first letter of every word in the given `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.charAt
text: Returns the `INTEGER` value of the character at the given index.

name: apoc.text.clean
text: Strips the given `STRING` of everything except alpha numeric characters and converts it to lower case.

name: apoc.text.code
text: Converts the `INTEGER` value into a `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.compareCleaned
text: Compares two given `STRING` values stripped of everything except alpha numeric characters converted to lower case.

name: apoc.text.decapitalize
text: Turns the first letter of the given `STRING` from upper case to lower case.

name: apoc.text.decapitalizeAll
text: Turns the first letter of every word in the given `STRING` to lower case.

name: apoc.text.distance
text: Compares the two given `STRING` values using the Levenshtein distance algorithm.

name: apoc.text.doubleMetaphone
text: Returns the double metaphone phonetic encoding of all words in the given `STRING` value.

name: apoc.text.format
text: Formats the given `STRING` with the given parameters.
signature: apoc.text.format(text :: STRING, params :: LIST<ANY>, language = en :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.fuzzyMatch
text: Performs a fuzzy match search of the two given `STRING` values.

name: apoc.text.hammingDistance
text: Compares the two given `STRING` values using the Hamming distance algorithm.

name: apoc.text.hexCharAt
text: Returns the hexadecimal value of the given `STRING` at the given index.

name: apoc.text.hexValue
text: Returns the hexadecimal value of the given value.

name: apoc.text.indexOf
text: Returns the first occurrence of the lookup `STRING` in the given `STRING`, or -1 if not found.
signature: apoc.text.indexOf(text :: STRING, lookup :: STRING, from = 0 :: INTEGER, to = -1 :: INTEGER) :: INTEGER

name: apoc.text.indexesOf
text: Returns all occurrences of the lookup `STRING` in the given `STRING`, or an empty list if not found.
signature: apoc.text.indexesOf(text :: STRING, lookup :: STRING, from = 0 :: INTEGER, to = -1 :: INTEGER) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.text.jaroWinklerDistance
text: Compares the two given `STRING` values using the Jaro-Winkler distance algorithm.

name: apoc.text.join
text: Joins the given `STRING` values using the given delimiter.

name: apoc.text.levenshteinDistance
text: Compares the given `STRING` values using the Levenshtein distance algorithm.

name: apoc.text.levenshteinSimilarity
text: Returns the similarity (a value within 0 and 1) between the two given `STRING` values based on the Levenshtein distance algorithm.

name: apoc.text.lpad
text: Left pads the given `STRING` by the given width.
signature: apoc.text.lpad(text :: STRING, count :: INTEGER, delimiter =   :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.phonetic
text: Returns the US_ENGLISH phonetic soundex encoding of all words of the `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.random
text:
Generates a random `STRING` to the given length using a length parameter and an optional `STRING` of valid characters.
Unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.
signature: apoc.text.random(length :: INTEGER, valid = A-Za-z0-9 :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.regexGroups
text: Returns all groups matching the given regular expression in the given text.

name: apoc.text.regexGroupsByName
text: Returns all groups with their group name matching the given regular expression in the given text.

name: apoc.text.regreplace
text: Finds and replaces all matches found by the given regular expression with the given replacement.
signature: apoc.text.regreplace(text :: STRING, regex :: STRING, replacement :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.repeat
text: Returns the result of the given item multiplied by the given count.

name: apoc.text.replace
text: Finds and replaces all matches found by the given regular expression with the given replacement.
signature: apoc.text.replace(text :: STRING, regex :: STRING, replacement :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.rpad
text: Right pads the given `STRING` by the given width.
signature: apoc.text.rpad(text :: STRING, count :: INTEGER, delimiter =   :: STRING) :: STRING

name: apoc.text.slug
text: Replaces the whitespace in the given `STRING` with the given delimiter.

name: apoc.text.snakeCase
text: Converts the given `STRING` to snake case.

name: apoc.text.sorensenDiceSimilarity
text: Compares the two given `STRING` values using the Sørensen–Dice coefficient formula, with the provided IETF language tag.
signature: apoc.text.sorensenDiceSimilarity(text1 :: STRING, text2 :: STRING, languageTag = en :: STRING) :: FLOAT

name: apoc.text.split
text: Splits the given `STRING` using a given regular expression as a separator.
signature: apoc.text.split(text :: STRING, regex :: STRING, limit = 0 :: INTEGER) :: LIST<ANY>

name: apoc.text.swapCase
text: Swaps the cases in the given `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.toCypher
text: Converts the given value to a Cypher property `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.toUpperCase
text: Converts the given `STRING` to upper case.

name: apoc.text.upperCamelCase
text: Converts the given `STRING` to upper camel case.

name: apoc.text.urldecode
text: Decodes the given URL encoded `STRING`.

name: apoc.text.urlencode
text: Encodes the given URL `STRING`.

name: apoc.util.md5
text:
Returns the MD5 checksum of the concatenation of all `STRING` values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.
MD5 is a weak hashing algorithm which is unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.

name: apoc.util.sha1
text:
Returns the SHA1 of the concatenation of all `STRING` values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.
SHA1 is a weak hashing algorithm which is unsuitable for cryptographic use-cases.

name: apoc.util.sha256
text: Returns the SHA256 of the concatenation of all `STRING` values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.util.sha384
text: Returns the SHA384 of the concatenation of all `STRING` values in the given `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.util.sha512
text: Returns the SHA512 of the concatenation of all `STRING` values in the `LIST<ANY>`.

name: apoc.util.validatePredicate
text: If the given predicate is true an exception is thrown, otherwise it returns true (for use inside `WHERE` subclauses).
signature: apoc.util.validatePredicate(predicate :: BOOLEAN, message :: STRING, params :: LIST<ANY>) :: BOOLEAN

name: apoc.version
text: Returns the APOC version currently installed.

name: apoc.xml.parse
text: Parses the given XML `STRING` as a `MAP`.
signature: apoc.xml.parse(data :: STRING, path = / :: STRING, config = {} :: MAP, simple = false :: BOOLEAN) :: MAP
